description: Two independent failures happened within minutes of each other, making it ambiguous which one caused the user-visible outage.

input.summary: Checkout flow broke at 13:10 UTC. Users see 'Unable to process payment' errors. Two things happened nearly simultaneously: a database failover completed at 13:08 and a payment provider certificate was rotated at 13:09.
input.logs:
13:08:00 WARN  [db] Primary database failover initiated (planned maintenance)
13:08:15 INFO  [db] Failover complete: new primary is db-replica-2
13:08:16 WARN  [db] 12 in-flight transactions were rolled back during failover
13:09:00 INFO  [payment] TLS certificate rotated for payment gateway endpoint
13:09:01 ERROR [payment] SSL handshake failed: certificate chain incomplete
13:09:02 ERROR [payment] SSL handshake failed: certificate chain incomplete
13:10:00 ERROR [checkout] Payment failed for order #88421: upstream error
13:10:01 ERROR [checkout] Payment failed for order #88422: upstream error
13:10:30 INFO  [db] All application connections re-established to new primary
input.metrics: Checkout success rate: 99.8% → 0%. DB failover duration: 15 seconds. Payment SSL errors: 0 → 100%. DB connections re-established by 13:10:30. Payment SSL errors persist after 13:10:30.
input.additionalContext: The DB failover was planned maintenance with a 15-second window. The certificate rotation was automated via cert-manager. Both events were expected but their proximity was coincidental.

plausibleCauses: Database failover caused rolled-back transactions and brief connection loss, breaking in-flight checkouts
Incomplete TLS certificate chain after rotation broke all payment gateway communication
Both failures compounded: DB failover disrupted initial requests, then payment cert errors prevented recovery
expectedBehavior.keyDiscriminator: The DB connections were re-established by 13:10:30 but payment SSL errors persisted, suggesting the certificate issue is the ongoing cause even if the DB failover contributed to the initial impact.
expectedBehavior.idealSynthesizerBehavior: Should present both causes, note the timeline overlap, and recommend fixing the cert chain first since it is the persisting failure, while confirming DB connectivity is restored.
